perturbation.original_text: Grantor hereby grants to Grantee and Grantee hereby accepts a franchise to construct, reconstruct, operate, maintain, repair, and extend a Gas Distribution System within Grantor's Territorial Limits in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth below ("Franchise"). The Franchise is granted pursuant to Grantor's Franchise Ordinance (Ordinance No. 967), adopted November 17, 2015, ("Ordinance"), which is incorporated by reference herein, including any applicable definitions.
perturbation.changed_text: Grantor hereby grants to Grantee and Grantee hereby accepts a permission to construct, reconstruct, operate, maintain, repair, and extend a Gas Distribution System within Grantor's Territorial Limits in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth below ("Franchise"). The Franchise is granted pursuant to Grantor's Franchise Ordinance (Ordinance No. 967), adopted November 17, 2015, ("Ordinance"), which is incorporated by reference herein, including any applicable definitions.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'franchise' has been replaced with 'permission', creating ambiguity. While the subsequent sentence refers to 'The Franchise', it's unclear if 'permission' and 'Franchise' are synonymous. If they are not, then the grant provided by Grantor is unclear. This creates an in-text contradiction because it first grants a 'permission', but proceeds as if a 'Franchise' was granted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Grantor hereby grants to Grantee and Grantee hereby accepts a permission to construct, reconstruct, operate, maintain, repair, and extend a Gas Distribution System within Grantor's Territorial Limits in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth below ("Franchise"). The Franchise is granted pursuant to Grantor's Franchise Ordinance (Ordinance No. 967), adopted November 17, 2015, ("Ordinance"), which is incorporated by reference herein, including any applicable definitions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'franchise' with 'permission' but retains the reference to 'The Franchise' in the following sentence. This creates a direct conflict because 'permission' and 'Franchise' are not explicitly defined as synonymous, leading to uncertainty about the actual grant provided by the Grantor. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, specifically when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The contradiction is strong because it impacts the core agreement (franchise vs. permission) and its enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Grantee shall pay to Grantor a Franchise Fee which shall be calculated pursuant to this Section. It is understood that Grantee has or will enter into franchise agreements with the City of Roanoke ("Roanoke") and the City of Salem ("Salem") and the Town of Vinton ("Vinton") (Grantor, Roanoke and Salem being hereinafter sometimes collectively referred to as the "localities" and singularly as a "locality") with fee provisions identical to this one, and that the total annual Franchise Fee to be paid to the three localities in aggregate is $98,196 for calendar year 2016 ("base year total annual Franchise Fee"). Grantor's Franchise Fee shall be a percentage share of the base year total annual Franchise Fee, which shall be determined on a pro rata basis according to its percentage share of the total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales occurring within the localities during the calendar year.
perturbation.changed_text: Grantee shall pay to Grantor a Municipal Fee which shall be calculated pursuant to this Section. It is understood that Grantee has or will enter into franchise agreements with the City of Roanoke ("Roanoke") and the City of Salem ("Salem") and the Town of Vinton ("Vinton") (Grantor, Roanoke and Salem being hereinafter sometimes collectively referred to as the "localities" and singularly as a "locality") with fee provisions identical to this one, and that the total annual Franchise Fee to be paid to the three localities in aggregate is $98,196 for calendar year 2016 ("base year total annual Franchise Fee"). Grantor's Municipal Fee shall be a percentage share of the base year total annual Franchise Fee, which shall be determined on a pro rata basis according to its percentage share of the total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales occurring within the localities during the calendar year.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Franchise Fee' with 'Municipal Fee' introduces inconsistency. While the section begins discussing the 'Municipal Fee', it later refers to the 'base year total annual Franchise Fee'. This contradiction creates uncertainty about what fee is actually being calculated and paid. It also creates contradiction with the definition being referenced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Grantee shall pay to Grantor a Municipal Fee which shall be calculated pursuant to this Section. It is understood that Grantee has or will enter into franchise agreements with the City of Roanoke ("Roanoke") and the City of Salem ("Salem") and the Town of Vinton ("Vinton") (Grantor, Roanoke and Salem being hereinafter sometimes collectively referred to as the "localities" and singularly as a "locality") with fee provisions identical to this one, and that the total annual Franchise Fee to be paid to the three localities in aggregate is $98,196 for calendar year 2016 ("base year total annual Franchise Fee"). Grantor's Municipal Fee shall be a percentage share of the base year total annual Franchise Fee, which shall be determined on a pro rata basis according to its percentage share of the total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales occurring within the localities during the calendar year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Franchise Fee' with 'Municipal Fee', but the contradicted section retains 'base year total annual Franchise Fee'. This creates a contradiction because the document now uses two different terms for what appears to be the same fee calculation, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability due to misaligned terminology, as defined in the prompt.

perturbation.original_text: Grantee shall during the term of this Franchise maintain at least one business office within the Territorial Limits of Grantor. Such office shall be open at least forty (40) hours per week for the conduct of business between Grantee and its customers.
perturbation.changed_text: Grantee shall during the term of this agreement maintain at least one service location within the Territorial Limits of Grantor. Such office shall be open at least forty (40) hours per week for the conduct of business between Grantee and its customers.
perturbation.explanation: The document now uses 'agreement' and 'office'. Although 'agreement' seems to refer to the entire contract, whereas office may have been replaced and assumed to be 'service location'. This creates ambiguity because it is uncler if the document is reffering to the franchise agreement that this has been provided or a different one. This in-text contradiction creates uncertanty on what 'agreement' is being reffered to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Grantee shall during the term of this agreement maintain at least one service location within the Territorial Limits of Grantor. Such office shall be open at least forty (40) hours per week for the conduct of business between Grantee and its customers.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Franchise' with 'agreement' while still using 'office' in the same sentence. The contradicted text also uses 'agreement' and 'office'. The perturbation explanation points out the ambiguity introduced by using 'agreement' which is undefined and creates uncertainty. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, specifically the use of multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The term 'agreement' now has uncertain meaning. This uncertainty directly conflicts with the need for clarity in the contract's terms, establishing a strong in-text contradiction.
